status_notes: Initial research from Google Cloud SQL documentation, 2026-04-04. Needs cross-reference and live environment validation.
notes: Managed relational databases (MySQL, PostgreSQL, SQL Server). Security includes encryption, IAM authentication, private IP, automated backups.

match_nodes.description: Data encryption at rest with Google-managed or customer-managed keys

data.description: Data encrypted at rest with Google-managed keys by default. Customer-managed encryption keys (CMEK) available via Cloud KMS for additional control. CMEK must be configured at instance creation time.

options.name: Google-Managed Keys
options.description: Default. Google manages encryption keys. No additional configuration.

options.name: Customer-Managed Keys (CMEK)
options.description: Customer manages keys in Cloud KMS. Provides key rotation control and ability to revoke access.

default.note: All data is encrypted at rest with Google-managed keys by default.

data.vendor_recommendation: Use CMEK for regulatory compliance and to maintain control over encryption key lifecycle.

match_nodes.description: Use IAM for database login with OAuth 2.0 tokens

data.description: Use IAM for database login. Generates OAuth 2.0 tokens instead of passwords. Supported for PostgreSQL and MySQL. Enables centralized identity management through Cloud IAM.

default.note: IAM database authentication must be explicitly enabled.

data.vendor_recommendation: Enable IAM authentication to eliminate long-lived database passwords and leverage centralized IAM policies.

match_nodes.description: Instance accessible only via VPC private IP

data.description: Instance accessible only via VPC private IP address. Removes public IP exposure. Uses VPC peering to the Google-managed network hosting Cloud SQL instances.

options.name: Private IP Only
options.description: No public IP assigned. Only reachable from VPC. Recommended.

options.name: Public IP
options.description: Public IP assigned. Reachable from internet (subject to authorized networks).

options.name: Both
options.description: Both private and public IP assigned.

data.vendor_recommendation: Use private IP only. Do not assign a public IP to production instances.

match_nodes.description: IP allowlist for public IP access

data.description: IP allowlist for public IP access. Specifies which external IP addresses or CIDR ranges can connect to the Cloud SQL instance. Only relevant when public IP is enabled.

default.note: No IP addresses are allowed by default. Connections are denied until authorized networks are configured.

data.vendor_recommendation: Minimize the number of authorized networks. Prefer private IP over authorized networks for production workloads.

match_nodes.description: Require SSL/TLS connections to Cloud SQL instances

data.description: Require SSL/TLS connections. When enabled, unencrypted connections are rejected. Cloud SQL supports server certificates and optional client certificates for mutual TLS.

options.name: Allow Unencrypted
options.description: Default. Both encrypted and unencrypted connections accepted.

options.name: Require SSL
options.description: Only encrypted connections accepted. Unencrypted connections rejected.

default.note: Unencrypted connections are allowed by default.

data.vendor_recommendation: Require SSL for all database connections.

data.description: Point-in-time recovery via automated backups. Retention period configurable from 1 to 365 days. Backups are taken daily during a configurable backup window.

default.note: Default backup retention period is 7 days.

data.vendor_recommendation: Enable automated backups with at least 7-day retention. Increase retention for compliance-sensitive workloads.

match_nodes.description: Prevent accidental deletion of Cloud SQL instances

data.description: Prevent accidental deletion of Cloud SQL instances. When enabled, the instance cannot be deleted until deletion protection is explicitly disabled.

default.note: Deletion protection is not enabled by default.

data.vendor_recommendation: Enable deletion protection for all production instances.

match_nodes.description: Control when patches and maintenance updates are applied

data.description: Control when patches and maintenance updates are applied. Security relevant because timely patching addresses known vulnerabilities. Configure preferred day and hour for maintenance.
data.vendor_recommendation: Set a maintenance window during off-peak hours. Subscribe to maintenance notifications. Do not defer maintenance indefinitely.
